# Transcrição: 01 Lesson #3 Understanding Business Manager

- **Fonte:** 01 Lesson #3 Understanding Business Manager
- **Duração:** 02:46
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:46]

This is a really important tool that is going to be very, very critical and foundational to everything that you do going forward as you grow your Facebook ads business. And you know what, even if you're not planning on working with clients even for your own self, if you ever work with agencies or contractors, Business Manager allows you to handle your own assets and give access to people and various teams without actually handing out passwords and without them having to use their personal Facebook profile to do so. So it's a critical piece to building a successful Facebook agency. Now all it does, literally all it does is it just brokers and manages relationships between your Facebook assets and your team. So here's what I mean by that. You have pages, you've got business pages and you've got ad accounts and you've got team members. There's a lot of other stuff down here which I don't want you to get too concerned about at this stage. This is a bit more advanced. But for now, you've got pages and ad accounts and you may have a couple people on your team or contractors. What Business Manager allows you to do is to connect all the pages of your clients or your own pages, all the ad accounts, add all your team members or contractors and you can assign different people to different assets and give them different levels of access. So some of them may only have analyst access to certain pages, some of them may be advertisers. This means that there's one hub for you to manage all of these pages and ad accounts. If you think about it in the past before Business Manager, what you used to have to do is request access to a client's page or ad account from your personal Facebook profile and then they would have to approve it and then when you logged into your personal profile, you had all the different pages that you had access to. The alternative of that was that they gave you login details, so password information. So it really wasn't the best way to do this. It either meant that you had their passwords, which is not a safe way for any business. It's too risky or it meant that your personal profile was cluttered with all of your clients' pages. So this is a way to remove that. You can add people with their work email addresses, not their personal profiles and you can have a hub where you manage all of that. That is simply all that Business Manager does. Now Business Manager has its own URL. It's business.facebook.com and if you watch the videos below, I show you exactly how to access it and how to create one for yourself.
